title: Document arrived: Q4-2025 Quarterly Filing — Meridian Logistics SA
detail: 3 pages · dated 2026-02-09 · Sentinel waking up

title: Triage
detail: Nemotron Nano classifies the arrival before waking the full agent

title: Triage: quarterly filing → investigate
detail: The filing reports a significant write‑off, new revolving facility draw, and liquidity levels, all of which affect covenant monitoring.

payload.reason: The filing reports a significant write‑off, new revolving facility draw, and liquidity levels, all of which affect covenant monitoring.

title: Planning
detail: Sentinel drafts its investigation plan

title: Investigation plan
detail: Assess credit deterioration, covenant compliance, and liquidity risk after Q4-2025 filing

payload.goal: Assess credit deterioration, covenant compliance, and liquidity risk after Q4-2025 filing
steps: Verify leverage and interest cover covenants using LTM EBITDA, net debt, and interest paid
Validate the EUR 3m revolver draw against facility headroom and use-of-proceeds
Evaluate liquidity runway and status of bank discussions or forbearance
Investigate Baltika write-off impact on receivables quality and revenue concentration
Review restructuring advisory scope and potential for broader balance-sheet restructuring
Check intercreditor position and collateral coverage given total borrowings exceed facility size

title: Investigating
detail: Multi-step evidence gathering: documents, ratios, ledger

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: I need to verify the exact covenant thresholds from the executed facility documentation before computing ratios. The filing gives me the financial inputs (net debt EUR 85.6m, LTM EBITDA EUR 19.4m, LTM interest EUR 4.6m, LTM scheduled principal EUR 4.0m), but I must not compute ratios manually. First step: search the credit agreement for the leverage and interest cover/DSCR covenant clauses so I can read the exact threshold page next.

title: search_documents
detail: {"query":"leverage ratio covenant threshold interest cover DSCR","docType":"credit-agreement-2023"}

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: The search returned page 7 of the credit agreement containing Section 7.1 Financial Covenants. I can see the leverage threshold is 3.50:1.00, but the DSCR/interest cover threshold is truncated. I must read the full page to capture the exact covenant language and thresholds before calling calc_ratio.

detail:
{
 "docId": "credit-agreement-2023",
 "docTitle": "Senior Facilities Agreement (2023)",
 "page": 7,
 "heading": "SECTION 7.1 — FINANCIAL COVENANTS",
 "text": "SECTION 7.1 — FINANCIAL COVENANTS\n\n7.1(a) Leverage. The Borrower shall ensure that the Leverage Ratio in respect of any Measurement Period ending on or after 30 June 2023 does not exceed 3.50:1.00.\n\n7.1(b) Debt Service Coverage. The Borr …[truncated 412 chars]

payload.heading: SECTION 7.1 — FINANCIAL COVENANTS
payload.text:
SECTION 7.1 — FINANCIAL COVENANTS

7.1(a) Leverage. The Borrower shall ensure that the Leverage Ratio in respect of any Measurement Period ending on or after 30 June 2023 does not exceed 3.50:1.00.

7.1(b) Debt Service Coverage. The Borrower shall ensure that the ratio of Consolidated EBITDA to Debt Service in respect of any Measurement Period is not less than 1.25:1.00.

The financial covenants in this Section 7.1 shall be tested quarterly by reference to each Measurement Period ending on the last day of each Financial Quarter, on the basis of the quarterly financial statements and the related Compliance Certificate.

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: I have read the exact covenant clauses on page 7: leverage must not exceed 3.50:1.00 and DSCR must not be less than 1.25:1.00. The Q4-2025 filing provides the financial inputs (net debt EUR 85,600k, LTM EBITDA EUR 19,400k, LTM interest paid EUR 4,600k, LTM scheduled principal EUR 4,000k). Per the hard rule I must not compute ratios myself. I will now call calc_ratio for the leverage covenant for Q4-2025.

title: calc_ratio
detail: {"metric":"leverage","quarter":"Q4-2025"}

title: calc_ratio → result
Sentinel reasons
detail: Leverage ratio is 4.41x, breaching the 3.50x threshold. I must now calculate the DSCR for Q4-2025 to check the second covenant before querying the ledger for causal evidence.

title: calc_ratio
detail: {"metric":"dscr","quarter":"Q4-2025"}

title: calc_ratio → result

payload.formula: Consolidated EBITDA (LTM) / (LTM interest paid + LTM scheduled principal)

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: I have verified both covenants against the exact clause on page 7 of the credit agreement: leverage must not exceed 3.50x (calc_ratio returned 4.41x — breach) and DSCR must not be less than 1.25x (calc_ratio returned 2.26x — compliant). Before drafting the escalation memo, I must attribute causes by surfacing material ledger movements for the tested quarter (Q4-2025) to obtain traceable evidence for the memo.

rows.description: Write-off — trade receivables — Baltika Retail Group insolvency

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: I have verified the exact covenant thresholds on page 7 of the credit agreement (leverage ≤3.50x, DSCR ≥1.25x), computed both ratios via calc_ratio (leverage 4.41x — breach; DSCR 2.26x — compliant), and surfaced material Q4-2025 ledger movements with a single query_ledger call. The evidence chain is complete: the filing pages are already in the log, the clause is on credit-agreement-2023 page 7, the ratios are tool-calculated, and the ledger traces the EUR 3m revolver draw, EUR 1.6m capex, EUR 1.45m Baltika write-off, and EUR 1.14m interest payment. Per the mandate to be economical, I now have sufficient evidence to draft the escalation-grade memo without further tool calls.

title: Drafting memo
detail: Writing memo from the investigation log

title: Draft memo: BREACH
detail: 5 findings · 7/7 citations verified verbatim

payload.title: Covenant Monitoring Memo — Meridian Logistics SA (Q4-2025)
payload.summary: Q4-2025 leverage is 4.41x, breaching the 3.50x ceiling under Section 7.1(a) of the Senior Facilities Agreement. DSCR of 2.26x remains above the 1.25x floor. The breach is driven by the EUR 1.45m Baltika receivables write-off and a EUR 3m revolver draw that increased net debt to EUR 85.6m against depressed LTM EBITDA of EUR 19.4m. Liquidity is tight at EUR 2.6m, with management in active bank discussions.

findings.text: The Leverage Ratio for Q4-2025 is 4.41x, exceeding the 3.50:1.00 threshold under Section 7.1(a).

citations.quote: The Borrower shall ensure that the Leverage Ratio in respect of any Measurement Period ending on or after 30 June 2023 does not exceed 3.50:1.00.

Total net debt: EUR 85,600 thousand
LTM EBITDA (last twelve months): EUR 19,400 thousand

findings.text: The Debt Service Coverage Ratio is 2.26x, remaining above the 1.25:1.00 minimum under Section 7.1(b).

citations.quote: The Borrower shall ensure that the ratio of Consolidated EBITDA to Debt Service in respect of any Measurement Period is not less than 1.25:1.00.

LTM interest paid: EUR 4,600 thousand
LTM scheduled principal repayments: EUR 4,000 thousand

findings.text: In October 2025 the Group drew EUR 3,000 thousand under the Revolving Facility, allocating EUR 1,600 thousand to the Lyon hub retrofit and the balance to working capital.

citations.quote: The Group drew a further EUR 3,000 thousand under the Revolving Facility in October 2025 (reference REVOLVER-DRAW-1002) to fund the second phase of the fleet electrification programme — warehouse charging retrofit at the Lyon hub for EUR 1,600 thousand (reference EQUIP-PURCH-1015) — and general working capital.

findings.text: The insolvency of Baltika Retail Group, the fourth-largest customer, caused a EUR 1,450 thousand trade receivables write-off and eliminated associated contracted volumes.

citations.quote: the insolvency of Baltika Retail Group, the Group's fourth-largest customer, resulting in a EUR 1,450 thousand write-off of trade receivables (reference WRITEOFF-1107, November 2025) and the loss of associated contracted volumes.

findings.text: Quarter-end liquidity was EUR 2,600 thousand, and management is in discussions with relationship banks to strengthen the position.

citations.quote: Quarter-end liquidity stood at EUR 2,600 thousand. Management is in discussions with its relationship banks regarding options to strengthen the liquidity position.

flaggedTransactions.cause: Fleet electrification and general working capital
Write-off — trade receivables — Baltika Retail Group insolvency

payload.recommendation: Classify the leverage breach as an Event of Default under Section 8.2 of the Senior Facilities Agreement. Demand an immediate Compliance Certificate and convene the credit committee to discuss forbearance, waiver, or amendment terms. Treat EUR 2.6m quarter-end liquidity as a critical watch item pending bank discussion outcomes.

title: The Skeptic reviews (round 1)
detail: Adversarial internal challenge before anything reaches a human

title: Objection OBJ-1: Covenant threshold verification
detail: The investigation log does not verify whether any Amendment Agreement modifies the covenant thresholds in the original Senior Facilities Agreement. Amendments prevail over the original agreement per the checklist, and the log only searched and read the original credit-agreement-2023, not any subsequent amendment documents.

payload.objection: The investigation log does not verify whether any Amendment Agreement modifies the covenant thresholds in the original Senior Facilities Agreement. Amendments prevail over the original agreement per the checklist, and the log only searched and read the original credit-agreement-2023, not any subsequent amendment documents.
payload.requiredAction: Search for any Amendment Agreement(s) to the Senior Facilities Agreement (2023) and, if found, read the relevant pages to confirm whether the leverage or DSCR thresholds have been amended for the Q4-2025 testing period.

title: Objection OBJ-2: Completeness of material movements
detail: The log shows a net debit movement of EUR -10,455,697 and credit movement of EUR 6,752,679 in Q4-2025, but only EUR 7,185,000 of the debits (the sum of the four flagged transactions) are matched to documented causes. The remaining EUR 3,270,697 of net debit movements (including payroll runs totaling EUR 4,849,771 and other items) are not explained or carried as outstanding items with follow-up actions.

payload.objection: The log shows a net debit movement of EUR -10,455,697 and credit movement of EUR 6,752,679 in Q4-2025, but only EUR 7,185,000 of the debits (the sum of the four flagged transactions) are matched to documented causes. The remaining EUR 3,270,697 of net debit movements (including payroll runs totaling EUR 4,849,771 and other items) are not explained or carried as outstanding items with follow-up actions.
payload.requiredAction: Either match the remaining material unexplained movements to documented causes from the filing or ledger, or explicitly carry them as outstanding items with a follow-up action in the memo.

title: Resolving objections
detail: Sentinel goes back to the evidence

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: OBJ-1 is a hard blocker: if an Amendment Agreement modified the leverage or DSCR thresholds, the breach conclusion could be invalid. I must search for amendments to the Senior Facilities Agreement (2023) before finalizing the memo. OBJ-2 (unexplained payroll and other movements) can be addressed after I confirm the covenant thresholds, likely by noting them as verified operating outflows in a follow-up ledger query or as outstanding items. Next best action: search for amendment documents.

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: OBJ-1 is a hard blocker: Amendment Agreement No. 2 dated 20 June 2025 appears to modify the leverage covenant for the Measurement Period ending 31 December 2025 (Q4-2025). The search snippet on page 2 is truncated before stating the amended threshold. I must read the full text of that page to confirm the exact threshold that applies to Q4-2025 before I can finalize the breach conclusion. OBJ-2 will be addressed after this threshold is verified.

 "page": 2,
 "heading": "AMENDMENT AGREEMENT NO. 2 — OPERATIVE PROVISIONS",
 "text": "AMENDMENT AGREEMENT NO. 2 — OPERATIVE PROVISIONS\n\nClause 2.1 (Covenant Holiday). Notwithstanding Section 7.1(a) of the Agreement, for the Measurement Periods ending 30 September 2025 and 31 December 2025 only, the Borrower s …[truncated 620 chars]

payload.heading: AMENDMENT AGREEMENT NO. 2 — OPERATIVE PROVISIONS
payload.text:
AMENDMENT AGREEMENT NO. 2 — OPERATIVE PROVISIONS

Clause 2.1 (Covenant Holiday). Notwithstanding Section 7.1(a) of the Agreement, for the Measurement Periods ending 30 September 2025 and 31 December 2025 only, the Borrower shall ensure that the Leverage Ratio does not exceed 4.00:1.00.

Clause 2.2 (Reversion). For each Measurement Period ending on or after 31 March 2026, the ratio in Section 7.1(a) shall revert to 3.50:1.00 without further action of the parties.

Clause 2.3 (Minimum Liquidity Condition). During the period referred to in Clause 2.1, the Borrower shall maintain Cash and Cash Equivalents of not less than EUR 3,000,000, tested on the last day of each Financial Quarter.

Clause 2.4 This Amendment Agreement is a Finance Document and prevails over the Agreement to the extent of any inconsistency.

title: Sentinel reasons
detail: OBJ-1 resolved by reading amendment-2 page 2: leverage threshold is 4.00x for Q4-2025 and a EUR 3m minimum liquidity condition applies. Leverage at 4.41x still breaches; liquidity at EUR 2.6m also breaches. DSCR 2.26x is compliant. OBJ-2 resolved by carrying remaining material movements as outstanding items requiring follow-up reconciliation.

payload.title: Covenant Monitoring Memo — Meridian Logistics SA (Q4-2025)
payload.summary: Q4-2025 leverage is 4.41x, breaching the amended 4.00x ceiling for the period. The DSCR of 2.26x remains compliant. The Group also failed the EUR 3m minimum liquidity condition under Amendment No. 2, holding EUR 2.6m. Drivers include the EUR 1.45m Baltika write-off and a EUR 3m revolver draw that lifted net debt to EUR 85.6m against LTM EBITDA of EUR 19.4m. Management is in bank discussions.

findings.text: The Leverage Ratio for Q4-2025 is 4.41x, exceeding the amended 4.00:1.00 ceiling that applies under Amendment Agreement No. 2 for the quarter.

citations.quote: for the Measurement Periods ending 30 September 2025 and 31 December 2025 only, the Borrower shall ensure that the Leverage Ratio does not exceed 4.00:1.00.

Total net debt: EUR 85,600 thousand
LTM EBITDA (last twelve months): EUR 19,400 thousand

findings.text: The Debt Service Coverage Ratio is 2.26x, remaining above the 1.25:1.00 minimum under Section 7.1(b).

citations.quote: The Borrower shall ensure that the ratio of Consolidated EBITDA to Debt Service in respect of any Measurement Period is not less than 1.25:1.00.

LTM interest paid: EUR 4,600 thousand
LTM scheduled principal repayments: EUR 4,000 thousand

findings.text: The Group breached the EUR 3,000,000 minimum liquidity condition under Clause 2.3 of Amendment Agreement No. 2, reporting EUR 2,600 thousand.

citations.quote: the Borrower shall maintain Cash and Cash Equivalents of not less than EUR 3,000,000, tested on the last day of each Financial Quarter.
Cash and cash equivalents: EUR 2,600 thousand

findings.text: In October 2025 the Group drew EUR 3,000 thousand under the Revolving Facility, allocating EUR 1,600 thousand to the Lyon hub retrofit and the balance to working capital.

citations.quote: The Group drew a further EUR 3,000 thousand under the Revolving Facility in October 2025 (reference REVOLVER-DRAW-1002) to fund the second phase of the fleet electrification programme — warehouse charging retrofit at the Lyon hub for EUR 1,600 thousand (reference EQUIP-PURCH-1015) — and general working capital.

findings.text: The insolvency of Baltika Retail Group, the fourth-largest customer, caused a EUR 1,450 thousand trade receivables write-off and eliminated associated contracted volumes.

citations.quote: the insolvency of Baltika Retail Group, the Group's fourth-largest customer, resulting in a EUR 1,450 thousand write-off of trade receivables (reference WRITEOFF-1107, November 2025) and the loss of associated contracted volumes.

flaggedTransactions.cause: Fleet electrification and general working capital
Write-off — trade receivables — Baltika Retail Group insolvency

payload.recommendation: Treat the leverage breach as an Event of Default under Section 8.2 and the liquidity shortfall as a breach of Amendment No. 2 Clause 2.3. Demand an immediate Compliance Certificate and convene the credit committee to discuss forbearance or restructuring. Flag the remaining Q4 payroll and operating outflows (approx. EUR 3.3m) for reconciliation.
